plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-372 RP12.P02.M07.S13-RP12.P02.M09.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-372 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP12.P02 test hermes slice: the M07 Test And Golden Case Set service tail (the ten remaining service rows), the full M08 Hermes Evidence Packet service cycle (twenty-two service rows), and the M09 Claude Review Packet head (the first eight service rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H12), Claude review packet (C12), and CP00-373 handoff, without opening billing, proforma, invoice, or write-down runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-372 (Risk B, 40 units RP12.P02.M07.S13-RP12.P02.M09.S08) is a descriptor-only billing-core p02 test/hermes/claude slice. Independent read-only inspection of all in-scope implementation (registry.js, service.js, validators.js, index.js, test/model.test.js, contracts/billing-core-contract.json, scripts/validate-rp12-billing-core-contract.mjs, README.md) plus pack evidence (manifest, command-evidence, baseline, readiness, validation-report, plan) confirms the pack-level claims hold. Scope and distributions are internally consistent across baseline/manifest/plan/contract/registry: deliverable counts (16/2/4/4/8/2/4) and micro-title counts (10/22/8) sum to 40; first/last unit, upstream CP00-371, and next CP00-373 wiring all agree. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced at row level (descriptor_only/runtime_execution=false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included=false) and result level (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, all dispatch flags false). The pack-level production_ready claim is correctly FALSE (implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout); enterprise trust is not claimed from local validation and human approval remains gated for runtime opening. Claude stays a read-only reviewer (tools Read/Grep/Glob, claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false). Hermes patterns are used only for descriptor-only evidence/receipt handling (receipt_shape ..._no_write; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false), not product runtime. Review-hardening readiness is clean: auth preflight passed (exit 0, non-zero valid-JSON stdout), no invalid attempts, summary-only review correctly prohibited, forbidden tools absent; post-review artifacts (raw-output, receipt, claude-review-result, adjudication, construction-inspection) correctly do not pre-exist. One non-blocking P3 note: certain conditional no-leak guards in the CP372 descriptor validator target row keys absent from this slice's vocabulary, but unconditional per-row guards plus result-level attestations fully cover the boundary; this mirrors the already-closed CP365-CP371 packs. No P0/P1/P2 issues found.

findings.title: Conditional no-leak guards in CP372 descriptor validator are inert for this slice's row vocabulary
findings.details: validateBillingCoreCp372P02TestHermesSliceDescriptor includes a defensive block that, per micro-phase, checks specific descriptor row keys (tenant_scope_field, state_transition_map, validation_helper, fixture_model, model_unit_test, invalid_reference_test, hermes_model_summary, claude_model_review_prompt) for their no-leak/no-write sub-flags. CP00-372 covers the M07 Test-And-Golden tail, M08 Hermes Evidence, and M09 Claude Review micro-phases, whose row keys are service/test/hermes vocabulary (e.g., tenant_boundary_precheck, state_transition_enforcement, validation_error_mapping, integration_smoke_case) and do not match any of those guarded keys, so each `if (rows.X && ...)` short-circuits and never fires. This is not a boundary gap: the unconditional per-row guards (descriptor_only=true, runtime_execution=false, customer_safe_errors_only=true, raw_payload_included=false) are asserted for every row, and freezeCp372Result stamps result-level attestations (writes_product_state=false, no_real_data=true, exposes_*=false, leak_detected=false, permission_bypass_detected=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false). The pattern is identical to the already-closed CP365-CP371 packs.
findings.recommendation: No action required for CP00-372 closeout. Optionally, in a future hardening pass, make the intent explicit (comment that these are defense-in-depth for shared model-definition row vocabularies) or add a positive assertion that model-definition descriptor keys are not unexpectedly present in service/test/hermes/claude slices.
findings.post_review_disposition: non_blocking_acknowledged: consistent with established CP365-CP371 cross-pack pattern; pack remains descriptor-only within no-write/no-real-data boundaries; does not affect pack or goal closeout

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-372 billing core p02 test hermes slice descriptor pack
